blurb:
The Select is the kind of place that looks impressive the second you walk in. The Art Deco interior is genuinely cool, and the open kitchen and conservatory make the whole room feel alive. This is a great pick for a date night or a special dinner when you want the full experience, not just a meal.

The menu is modern European, and the kitchen takes it seriously. The food is creative without being weird about it, and the cocktail program is worth exploring too. It's on the pricier side, but the atmosphere and the quality of the food make it feel worth it.

They don't open for lunch on weekdays, so this is really a dinner destination. Sunday brunch starts at 10am if you want to try it in a more relaxed setting.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Suite 200, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 4:00âââ10:00â¯PM, Tuesday: 4:00âââ10:00â¯PM, Wednesday: 4:00âââ10:00â¯PM, Thursday: 4:00âââ11:00â¯PM, Friday: 4:00â¯PMâââ12:00â¯AM, Saturday: 4:00â¯PMâââ12:00â¯AM, Sunday: 10:00â¯AMâââ10:00â¯PM

blurb:
Royal Spice is the Indian place I'd send a friend to if they asked for a recommendation around Sandy Springs. The staff is helpful and happy to walk you through the menu, which matters when you're trying something new or figuring out what to order. The space is calm and quiet, good for a relaxed dinner.

The entrees are the main draw here, and the appetizers are worth getting too. People come back specifically to try more items, which says a lot. It's a good spot for a dinner out with family or a low-key date night.

They close between lunch and dinner on weekdays, so check the hours if you're planning around that gap.
address: 220 Hammond Dr #302, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Little Thai Cuisine is tucked into the Sandy Springs Circle shopping area and it's easy to walk past it, but don't. It's small and cozy inside, and the regulars here are regulars for a reason. Good for a low-key weeknight dinner or a quick lunch when you're already in that part of town.

The curries and stir-fries are what people come back for. It's straightforward Thai done well, and the portions are solid. The kind of place where you already know what you're going to order by the second visit.

One thing worth knowing: they break between lunch and dinner Monday through Friday, closing at 2:30pm and reopening at 5pm. Not the place to show up at 3:30 on a Tuesday.
address: 220 Sandy Springs Cir #209, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Rreal Tacos is one of those places that has the kind of buzz you're skeptical about until you actually go. Then you understand. The vibe is lively and the service is genuinely attentive, and the food backs up every bit of the hype.

Start with the Esquite before anything else. For drinks, the Pineapple Guava pitcher or the Passion Fruit pitcher are both solid and easy to share with a group. The tacos are the main event, and the portions are good. This is a great spot for a big group dinner, a girls night out, or just a fun weeknight when you want good food and a real margarita.

They're open until midnight most nights, which is a nice bonus when you don't feel like rushing.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00â¯AMâââ12:00â¯AM, Tuesday: 11:00â¯AMâââ12:00â¯AM, Wednesday: 11:00â¯AMâââ12:00â¯AM, Thursday: 11:00â¯AMâââ12:00â¯AM, Friday: 11:00â¯AMâââ12:00â¯AM, Saturday: 11:00â¯AMâââ12:00â¯AM, Sunday: 11:00â¯AMâââ11:00â¯PM

blurb:
If you haven't tried Persian food and you're looking for a reason to, Yalda is a great place to start. It's a beautiful space in Sandy Springs and the kind of restaurant that works just as well for a girls night out as it does for a date or a family dinner. People come back again and again, and the reviews show it.

The hummus and wings are a solid way to start the meal. The chicken shish kabobs with rice made with lentils and raisins is the dish people keep talking about, and it's worth ordering. Save room for the tiramisu at the end.

Note that there's a break between lunch and dinner service Monday through Friday, so plan accordingly if you're going on a weeknight.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
If you're looking for a special occasion spot that actually delivers, Canoe is the real deal. It sits right on the Chattahoochee River, and the setting is genuinely beautiful whether you're there for lunch or a weekend dinner. This is the kind of place you take out-of-town visitors when you want to show off Atlanta a little.

The menu is farm-to-table New American, which means the ingredients are fresh and the dishes change with the seasons. It's a splurge for sure, but the food backs it up. Think elevated comfort food done right, not fussy or precious about it.

They do Sunday brunch too, which is worth knowing. Reservations are a good idea, especially on weekends. This isn't a drop-in kind of place.
address: 4199 Paces Ferry Rd SE, Atlanta, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
Heirloom Market BBQ is a tiny place over on Akers Mill, and I mean tiny. There's basically one communal table. But people drive across town for this BBQ, and once you try it you'll understand why. It's a great pick for lunch on a weekday when you want something worth talking about.

What makes it different from regular BBQ joints is the slight Korean influence in some of the flavors. It's subtle, but it makes the whole thing feel more interesting than your average smoke-and-sauce setup.

They're only open Tuesday through Saturday, 11am to 8pm, and they do sell out. Get there on the earlier side if you want to make sure you don't miss out.
address: 2243 Akers Mill Rd SE, Atlanta, GA 30339, USA
hours: Monday: Closed, Tuesday: 11:00â¯AMâââ8:00â¯PM, Wednesday: 11:00â¯AMâââ8:00â¯PM, Thursday: 11:00â¯AMâââ8:00â¯PM, Friday: 11:00â¯AMâââ8:00â¯PM, Saturday: 11:00â¯AMâââ8:00â¯PM, Sunday: Closed

blurb:
Canton Cooks on Roswell Road is the kind of Chinese place that goes beyond the usual takeout menu. They do hot pot, BBQ duck, and lobster in black bean sauce, so if you're tired of the same old options, this is worth checking out. It's casual and open late, which makes it a good call for a weeknight dinner when you don't want to rush.

The hot pot is a fun option if you're going with a group and want something a little more interactive. The BBQ duck and lobster in black bean sauce are the standout dishes that set them apart from most spots in the area.

They're open every day until 10pm, so it works for a later dinner too.
address: 5984 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00â¯AMâââ10:00â¯PM, Tuesday: 11:00â¯AMâââ10:00â¯PM, Wednesday: 11:00â¯AMâââ10:00â¯PM, Thursday: 11:00â¯AMâââ10:00â¯PM, Friday: 11:00â¯AMâââ10:00â¯PM, Saturday: 12:00âââ10:00â¯PM, Sunday: 12:00âââ10:00â¯PM

blurb:
If you want a place that actually feels special, Ray's on the River is worth the drive down Powers Ferry. It sits right on the Chattahoochee and the view makes any dinner feel like an occasion. This is the spot for anniversaries, birthday dinners, or when you really want to impress someone.

The menu leans into Southern seafood, and the setting is more intimate than you might expect from a place this well-known. It's been around long enough that people trust it, and with over 11,000 reviews averaging 4.6 stars, that reputation holds up.

Prices are on the higher end, so go in knowing that. Sunday hours are split, with a break between 4pm and 5pm, so plan your reservation accordingly.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30â¯AMâââ10:00â¯PM, Tuesday: 11:30â¯AMâââ10:00â¯PM, Wednesday: 11:30â¯AMâââ10:00â¯PM, Thursday: 11:30â¯AMâââ10:00â¯PM, Friday: 11:30â¯AMâââ11:00â¯PM, Saturday: 12:00âââ11:00â¯PM, Sunday: 11:00â¯AMâââ4:00â¯PM, 5:00âââ9:00â¯PM

blurb:
Breadwinner is the kind of lunch spot you wish was closer to wherever you work. It's counter service, bright and casual, and the bread is genuinely the star of the show. Perfect for a weekday lunch or a quick stop on a Saturday before the afternoon gets away from you.

They do sandwiches, soups, and salads, all built around their housemade breads. Nothing complicated, just good food that fills you up without being heavy. If you're working from home and need to get out of the house for an hour, this is a solid option.

They close at 6pm on weekdays and 5pm on Saturdays, and they're closed Sundays, so this is purely a daytime spot.
address: 220 Sandy Springs Cir, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 10:30â¯AMâââ6:00â¯PM, Tuesday: 10:30â¯AMâââ6:00â¯PM, Wednesday: 10:30â¯AMâââ6:00â¯PM, Thursday: 10:30â¯AMâââ6:00â¯PM, Friday: 10:30â¯AMâââ6:00â¯PM, Saturday: 10:30â¯AMâââ5:00â¯PM, Sunday: Closed

blurb:
This place is a full experience, not just dinner. Thrill is a big, two-level Korean BBQ spot on Roswell Road and the energy in there on a weekend night is real. It's the kind of place you go when you want the night to actually feel like an event. Great for a group birthday, a night out with friends, or anyone who's never done Korean BBQ before.

They do all-you-can-eat Korean BBQ, so you're cooking your own meat at the table. The handcrafted cocktails are worth trying too. Friday and Saturday they're open until 2am, which makes it a rare option if you want a late dinner without settling.

Weekday dinner service doesn't start until 5pm, so this one is dinner only Monday through Thursday.
address: 5958 Roswell Rd, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 5:00â¯PMâââ12:00â¯AM, Tuesday: 5:00â¯PMâââ12:00â¯AM, Wednesday: 5:00â¯PMâââ12:00â¯AM, Thursday: 5:00â¯PMâââ12:00â¯AM, Friday: 11:00â¯AMâââ2:00â¯AM, Saturday: 11:00â¯AMâââ2:00â¯AM, Sunday: 11:00â¯AMâââ12:00â¯AM

blurb:
Sunnyside Pizzeria is the kind of neighborhood pizza place you feel good telling people about. It's in Sandy Springs and has a 4.7 rating across over a thousand reviews, which tells you this isn't a fluke. Good for a casual weeknight dinner, date night, or anytime you want pizza that actually lives up to the hype.

The Hot Honey Charred pizza is the one people keep coming back for. The Hot Honey Lemon Pepper Chicken Wings are worth starting with too, sweet, a little spicy, and apparently good enough to dip your crust into. The Caesar Salad gets mentioned a lot as well if you want something fresh on the side.

They're closed on Mondays, so plan accordingly.
address: 220 Sandy Springs Cir Suite 149, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: Closed, Tuesday: 11:30â¯AMâââ9:00â¯PM, Wednesday: 11:30â¯AMâââ9:00â¯PM, Thursday: 11:30â¯AMâââ9:00â¯PM, Friday: 11:30â¯AMâââ9:30â¯PM, Saturday: 12:00âââ9:30â¯PM, Sunday: 12:00âââ9:00â¯PM

blurb:
If you live anywhere near Roswell Road and want a solid pizza without driving far, JJ's is the move. It's a no-frills spot in a strip mall, but the pizza is the real deal. Good for a casual weeknight dinner or picking up a pie on a Friday night.

The stuffed pizza is what people are raving about right now, and honestly it earns it. One slice will fill you up, so plan accordingly. The regular pies are reliable too, and the prices are very reasonable for what you get.

Just note they're closed on Tuesdays, so don't make that mistake on a random weeknight like I almost did.
address: 2211 Roswell Rd suite 116, Marietta, GA 30062, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00â¯AMâââ9:00â¯PM, Tuesday: Closed, Wednesday: 11:00â¯AMâââ9:00â¯PM, Thursday: 11:00â¯AMâââ9:00â¯PM, Friday: 11:00â¯AMâââ10:00â¯PM, Saturday: 11:00â¯AMâââ10:00â¯PM, Sunday: 12:00âââ9:00â¯PM

blurb:
This little spot on Roswell Road is a gem. The owner is genuinely passionate about what they do, and people notice it. If you've never had real pho before, this is a good place to start. They're one of the few spots around that make it the traditional way, and it shows.

The pho is the thing to get. Reviewers say it's the real deal, not the watered-down version you find at a lot of places. Prices are affordable too, so it works great for a quick lunch or an easy weeknight dinner without spending much.

They're open every day from 10am to 9pm, which makes it one of the more flexible options in the area.
address: 5980 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 10:00â¯AMâââ9:00â¯PM, Tuesday: 10:00â¯AMâââ9:00â¯PM, Wednesday: 10:00â¯AMâââ9:00â¯PM, Thursday: 10:00â¯AMâââ9:00â¯PM, Friday: 10:00â¯AMâââ9:00â¯PM, Saturday: 10:00â¯AMâââ9:00â¯PM, Sunday: 10:00â¯AMâââ9:00â¯PM

blurb:
This little Brazilian spot on Roswell Road is something different, in the best way. It's a cozy, laid-back place where you build your own aÃ§aÃ­ bowl with as many toppings as you want. Good for an afternoon treat, post-workout snack, or just something fresh when you don't want another burger.

The self-serve aÃ§aÃ­ setup is fun, and the staff is genuinely friendly about helping you figure out what to try. They also have crepes and coxinha, which is a Brazilian chicken fritter, and both are worth ordering if you've never had them.

They're closed Tuesdays, so keep that in mind.
address: 2145 Roswell Rd Suite 110, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
If you want a solid breakfast or lunch spot that doesn't mess around, J. Christopher's in Sandy Springs is worth knowing about. It's a daytime-only place, open every day until 2pm, so it works great for a weekend brunch or a weekday lunch when you want something real. The vibe is casual and comfortable, not fancy.

They do classic breakfast stuff well, omelets, burgers, and they mix in seasonal specials that keep things interesting. It's the kind of place where you know what you're getting and it shows up the way you hoped.

Just keep in mind they close at 2pm every day, so don't try to sneak in for a late lunch.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 7:00â¯AMâââ2:00â¯PM, Tuesday: 7:00â¯AMâââ2:00â¯PM, Wednesday: 7:00â¯AMâââ2:00â¯PM, Thursday: 7:00â¯AMâââ2:00â¯PM, Friday: 7:00â¯AMâââ2:00â¯PM, Saturday: 7:00â¯AMâââ2:00â¯PM, Sunday: 7:00â¯AMâââ2:00â¯PM

blurb:
If you want a step above your standard pizza night, Hearth is a solid go-to on Roswell Road. The brick-lined interior feels comfortable without being fancy, and they have a nice patio if the weather cooperates. Good for a casual date night or a Friday dinner with friends.

The pies are inventive without being weird about it. This is the kind of place where you actually want to look at the menu instead of just ordering the usual. They have beer and wine to go with it, which makes it easy to turn pizza into a real night out.

They're open seven days a week through 10pm most nights, so it works well for a later weeknight dinner when you don't want to rush.
address: 5992 Roswell Rd NE, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00â¯AMâââ10:00â¯PM, Tuesday: 11:00â¯AMâââ10:00â¯PM, Wednesday: 11:00â¯AMâââ10:00â¯PM, Thursday: 11:00â¯AMâââ10:00â¯PM, Friday: 11:00â¯AMâââ10:00â¯PM, Saturday: 11:00â¯AMâââ10:00â¯PM, Sunday: 11:00â¯AMâââ9:00â¯PM

blurb:
Los Bravos is a go-to for a reliable Mexican meal on Roswell Road. The food from the kitchen is consistently good, and it works well for a family dinner or a casual night out. They're open every day and stay open late, which is handy.

The kitchen is the strong suit here. Longtime regulars tend to stick to the food and keep an eye on what they're ordering from the bar, since the margarita prices have crept up noticeably compared to other spots nearby.

Go for the food and you'll leave happy. Just know what you're getting into if you plan to order a round of drinks.
address: 2125 Roswell Rd #40, Marietta, GA 30062, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00â¯AMâââ10:00â¯PM, Tuesday: 11:00â¯AMâââ10:00â¯PM, Wednesday: 11:00â¯AMâââ10:00â¯PM, Thursday: 11:00â¯AMâââ10:30â¯PM, Friday: 11:00â¯AMâââ10:30â¯PM, Saturday: 11:00â¯AMâââ10:30â¯PM, Sunday: 11:00â¯AMâââ10:00â¯PM

blurb:
Brooklyn Cafe is one of those places you find yourself going back to more than you expect. It's warm and comfortable inside, with a nice patio when you want it. Works well for a lunch meeting, a relaxed dinner with the family, or a night out without needing a reservation weeks in advance.

The menu is American bistro style, the kind of food that feels familiar but a little more put-together than your average neighborhood spot. They have a solid wine list, which makes it easy to make a meal out of it. Locals have been coming here for years for a reason.

Just note they are closed on Sundays, so plan accordingly.
address: 220 Sandy Springs Cir, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
This place has a 4.9 rating with over 5,000 reviews, which is almost impossible for a pizza spot. But once you go, you get it. The pizza is thin-crust Italian style made with good ingredients, and it shows in every bite. It's the kind of pizza that makes you rethink your usual order.

They use locally sourced toppings alongside ingredients brought in from Italy, and the antipasti and salads are worth ordering too, not just an afterthought. Good for a weeknight dinner, a casual date night, or grabbing a table with the family on a Friday.

They're open every day starting at 11:30am and stay open until 11pm on Friday and Saturday, so there's no scramble to get there before the kitchen closes.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA
